description: In an apartment living room, Chandler sits on a sofa reading a newspaper while Joey stands at an open refrigerator in the kitchen area. Audience laughter is audible.
Joey looks inside the refrigerator. "All right, they got water, orange juice and what looks like cider," he says.
Chandler, looking up from his newspaper toward Joey, says, "Taste it."
Joey takes a glass from the refrigerator, drinks from it, then stops and grimaces, exhaling sharply.
Joey lowers the glass, turning toward Chandler. "Yep."
Placing his hands on his hips, Joey says, "It's fat. I drank fat."
Audience laughter is heard as Chandler remains seated, looking at Joey.
Chandler looks back down at his newspaper and says, "Yeah, I know. I did that two minutes ago."
Joey gestures with the glass and walks away from the refrigerator. Ross enters through the front door, wearing a tuxedo.
Joey points at Ross with the glass. "All right. Hey, Mr."
"Tux," Joey finishes. Ross steps further inside, spreading his arms. "Why aren't you guys dressed? We have a half hour," Ross asks.
Chandler replies, "No, four minutes ago you had a half hour." Ross points at his watch and walks past the sofa, saying, "We have to be out the door 20 to eight."
Chandler gestures with his hands. "Relax, Ross. We'll be ready. It only takes us two minutes to get dressed."
Ross stops pacing and turns to Chandler, gesturing with his hand. "But you know, I'd feel a whole lot better if you got dressed now."
Chandler, looking at his newspaper, says, "Okay."
Ross stands near the front door. Rachel emerges from a hallway, wearing a patterned bathrobe.
"Hey," Rachel says, walking into the room. "Oh, look at you all sexy," she adds, looking at Ross.
Ross looks at Rachel and says, "Really?"
Rachel smiles and says, "Wow."
Ross and Rachel step closer to each other, wrapping their arms around one another.
"Hi," they say simultaneously. Rachel rests her hands on Ross's chest and asks, "How come you didn't come over earlier?"
Ross leans in close to her face and says, "Because I'm a stupid, stupid man," his volume softer than his previous lines.
Joey approaches Ross from behind, holding the glass.
Joey holds out the glass. "Hey, Ross, want some cider?"
Ross turns his head slightly toward Joey without breaking his embrace. "No."
Joey steps back, looking down at the glass.
Ross holds Rachel's hands and looks at her. "So, let's see. You're pretty close."
"Makeup's on. Hair is done," Rachel says, nodding. "Yeah, I just have to get dressed," she adds. Ross says, "Yay. And that takes what? Just six or seven minutes?"
"Yeah, once I," Rachel begins, turning away and walking toward the hallway.
Rachel stops in the doorway and looks back. "figure out what I'm wearing," she says.
Ross clasps his hands together. Joey steps forward, holding the glass.
Joey holds up the glass toward Ross. "Glass of fat?"
Audience laughter plays as the scene fades to black.
The screen remains black.
The scene fades back to the apartment living room. Ross sits on the sofa, while Joey sits in an armchair nearby, eating from a small bowl.
Joey gestures toward Ross. "What's the matter, Ross? Are you nervous about your speech?"
Ross shakes his head slightly. "No."
Joey continues eating. Ross looks toward Joey.
Ross leans forward slightly. "Do you want to hear it?" Joey asks, "Am I in?"
Ross stares at Joey, his facial expression remaining neutral.
Joey stops eating and looks back at Ross. "Huh?"
Ross continues to look at Joey as audience laughter plays.
"Yeah. Right after I thank everyone for giving money to the museum I sing a song about the wonder that is Joey," Ross says, his pitch flat.
Joey smiles. Phoebe enters through the front door, wearing a yellow dress and carrying a coat and purse.
Joey looks at Phoebe and exclaims, "wow." "Hello," Phoebe says. "You look great," Ross tells her. "Thank you. I know, though," Phoebe replies.
Ross stands up and approaches Phoebe.
Ross gestures with his hands toward Phoebe. "You see this? This is a person who is ready to go."
Pointing at her, Ross says, "Phoebe, you are my star."
Phoebe smiles at Ross.
Phoebe rests her hand on Ross's arm. "Well, you're my lucky penny," she says.
Phoebe walks past Ross into the kitchen area. Chandler enters the room carrying a magazine.
Chandler holds up the magazine. "All right, I took the quiz, and it turns out I do put career before men," he says.
Chandler walks over to the armchair where Joey is sitting and stops in front of him.
Chandler gestures at Joey. "Get up."
Joey looks up at Chandler.
"What?" Joey asks. "You're my seat," Chandler states. "How is this your seat?" Joey asks, gesturing with a piece of food.
Chandler looks at Phoebe and Ross, who are standing nearby.
Chandler turns back to Joey. "Because I was sitting there, but then you left," he says.
Joey shrugs slightly.
Chandler leans forward, resting his hands on his hips. "Well, it's not like I went to Spain."
Joey continues eating, looking at Chandler.
"I went to the bathroom. You knew I was coming back," Chandler says, gesturing with one hand.
Joey raises his free hand. "What's the big deal? Sit somewhere else." Chandler points downward. "The big deal is I was sitting there last, so"
Chandler points firmly downward. "it's my seat."
Joey gestures with his hand toward the hallway.
"Actually, the last place you were sitting was in there. So," Joey says, pointing toward the hallway.
Chandler stares at Joey. Ross steps forward rapidly.
Ross steps between them, waving his hands. "you guys, you know what? You know what?"
Ross points at them. "It doesn't matter because you both have to go get dressed before the big vein in my head"
"pops," Ross finishes, tapping his forehead. Chandler gestures with both hands. "All right, Ross. I just have to do one thing really quickly. It's not a big deal."
Chandler turns back to Joey and yells, "Get up!"
Ross's eyes widen as he looks at Chandler.
In a living room and kitchen area, Phoebe stands near a refrigerator holding a water bottle. Ross enters wearing a tuxedo.
Ross approaches Phoebe and says, "Hi. OOH, phoebe, you look great." Phoebe replies, "Sounds right already."
Monica walks into the kitchen area holding a purple garment and stops near Ross.
Monica looks at Ross's tuxedo and asks, "OOH, are you going to do magic?"
Ross points at Monica and smiles slightly.
Ross lowers his hand and says, "That's funny."

Ross gestures toward Monica and says, "Change." Monica places a bag on the table and replies, "Hang on a second. I just got in." Ross points at her and says, "Look, I don't care. It starts at eight. We can't be late."
Phoebe, standing nearby, adds, "And he could not, would not want to wait."
Ross looks at Phoebe, then turns his attention back to Monica.
Ross gestures with his hands as he speaks to Monica. "Look, our table is down in front, okay?"

Ross continues, "My boss is going to be there. Everyone will see if we arrive after it starts." Monica picks up a glass containing a light-colored liquid from the counter and asks, "Has somebody been drinking my fat?"
Joey, sitting in an armchair in the living area, and Chandler, standing nearby, both look silently toward Monica.

Rachel walks into the room wearing a printed bathrobe, holding a black outfit on a hanger. She asks, "You guys, does this look like something the girlfriend of a paleontologist would wear?" Phoebe replies, "I don't know."
Phoebe adds, "You might be the first one." Ross points at Phoebe with a smile.
Monica points at Rachel from the kitchen counter.
Monica asks, "Rach, did you check the machine?" Rachel shakes her head and says, "No."
Rachel looks at the garment she is holding and says, "Wait. You know what? This is? The outfit that makes my calves look fat?"
Rachel says, "Never mind," and turns away.
Rachel walks back toward the hallway. Ross gestures with his hands as she leaves. Chandler stands near Joey, who eats from a small container with a spoon.
Chandler points to his head and then gestures to Joey. "Well, Joey, I wrote a little song today."
Chandler points down at Joey and says, "It's called get up."
Joey looks up at Chandler.
Joey gestures with his spoon and says, "Gee, you know, I wish there was something I could do to help you." Chandler bends toward him, raising his volume as he shouts, "Get up."
Chandler repeatedly shouts, "Get up. Get up. Get up. Get up. Get up. Get up. Get up," while moving quickly around Joey's chair.
Chandler finishes shouting, saying "Get up. Get up." Joey stands up and says, "All right, you can have the chair."
Chandler looks at Joey as he steps away from the armchair.
Chandler says, "Really?"
Joey stops and turns back around.
Joey points toward the window and says, "Oh, my. What you look at that." As Chandler turns to look, Joey quickly sits back down in the armchair.
Chandler turns back, realizes Joey is sitting, and stares at him. Ross points at them. Phoebe, sitting on the sofa, opens her purse.
An answering machine beeps and plays Phoebe's voice. "Hi, it's me. I'm coming over now." Phoebe looks toward the machine.
Phoebe smiles and points to herself as her recorded voice continues, "Hey, what if I'm already there when you're playing this message? Is that too spooky?"
Phoebe laughs briefly. Ross looks at his wristwatch.
The answering machine plays Ross's voice, "Hi, Rachel, you there? It's me."
Ross points toward the hallway as his recorded voice says, "Pick up."
Ross keeps his arm raised.
Ross's recorded voice says, "Rachel?"
Ross's recorded voice says, "Rach?" Rachel emerges from the hallway wearing her printed bathrobe.
Rachel looks at Ross and asks, "What?"
Ross's facial expression falls as he looks at Rachel's attire.
Ross lowers his hand and says, "Never mind."
The answering machine emits a beep.
The machine plays a woman's voice: "Hi, Rach. It's your sister. Did you ever sleep with Donnie Mazio?" Ross grasps Rachel's hands.
The recorded voice continues, "Because last night he kept shouting, Two for two." Rachel's eyes widen.
Ross shakes his head slightly while Rachel keeps her gaze on him.
Rachel releases Ross's hands and asks, "All right, you want me to get dressed or do you want to hear a really long story about the senior Hayride?"
From the armchair, Joey asks, "Are you only asking him because"
Ross spins around, throws his hands down, and glares at Joey. Joey raises his hands slightly.
Rachel turns and walks back down the hallway. Ross watches her go.
Monica stands in the kitchen reading a piece of mail. The answering machine plays a man's voice, "monica, it's Richard. Call me."
Monica freezes and stares ahead. Ross, Phoebe, and Chandler look toward her and the machine.
Monica steps forward, gesturing with her hands, and asks, "Is that message old or new?"
Monica repeatedly asks, "Old or new? Old or new?" her volume rising.
Ross steps quickly toward Monica.
Ross says, "It's definitely old. Didn't you hear the double beep?"
Monica gestures outward and replies, "Well, what if it's new? I mean, we agreed not to talk. He could have something"
Monica continues, "really important to say. Shouldn't I call him back?" Ross places his hand on her shoulder and says, "Honey, you did call him back, because it's really old."
Chandler points at Monica and adds, "Yeah. See, Mon, listen."
Ross places his other hand on Monica's shoulder.
Ross looks directly at Monica and says, "When Carol and I broke up, I went through the same thing."
Ross continues steadily, "And you know what I did? I got dressed"
Ross grips Monica's shoulders tightly.
Ross turns Monica toward the hallway and says, "it really, really quickly."
Ross pushes Monica down the hall, saying, "Okay, there we go. There we go."
Ross watches Monica walk away.
Chandler gestures with his arms and says, "You know what?"
Chandler points at Joey and says, "Okay, fine. Don't get up. You just sit right there. I just hope you don't mind my hand right here," holding his open palm in front of Joey's face.
Joey leans back slightly, looking at Chandler's hand.
Chandler moves his hand closer to Joey's face. "Oh, not touching. Can't get mad. Not touching. Can't get mad."
Chandler repeats, "Not touching."
Joey thrusts his spoon toward Chandler, and a piece of food flies off it.
Phoebe gasps, "Oh, my God."
Phoebe stands up, looking down at a large stain on her yellow dress. She says, "You rotten boys."
Chandler and Joey look at Phoebe.
Joey and Chandler simultaneously say, "Sorry, Petey. Sorry, Petey." Phoebe dabs at her dress and says, "No, don't rob it. Don't"
Ross runs over, waving his hands rapidly. "what gets out hummus? What gets out hummus? Monica."
Monica walks out of the hallway, fully dressed in a floral top and skirt. Ross turns to her and asks, "Monica. You know what gets out hummus?"
Monica ignores his question and asks, "If it is a new message, what is he calling to say?"
Phoebe rubs the stain on her dress, then looks up at Monica.
Phoebe smiles slightly and says, "Okay, thanks. Yeah, I'll try that."
Chandler takes a step forward.
Chandler says, "Maybe he's calling to say you're obsessive and crazy." Monica asks, "So should I call him back?"
Ross, Chandler, and Joey say in unison, "No."
Monica nods and turns around. Rachel walks out of the hallway holding a black and red outfit on a hanger, moving toward Ross.
Rachel holds up the outfit and asks, "Okay. Do these match?" Ross looks closely at the garment and replies, "Yes, they definitely match." Rachel lowers it and says, "They don't match."
Rachel turns and carries the outfit back toward the hallway as Ross stares after her.
In an apartment living room, Joey sits in an armchair while Chandler stands beside him. Ross stands nearby, wearing a tuxedo. Chandler throws his hands out. Audience laughter plays.

Chandler looks toward Ross and says, "What? They didn't match." Joey looks up and says, "All right, fine." Chandler gestures and says, "You know what? We'll both sit in the chair." Joey adds, "Fine with me." Chandler steps over Joey's legs and sits directly on Joey's lap.
Chandler rests his arms on the back of the chair, his legs spread wide over Joey's. Both men look forward as audience laughter is heard.
Chandler shifts slightly and says, "I am so comfortable."
Joey shifts his position beneath Chandler.
Joey looks up at Chandler and says, "Me too. In fact, I think I might be a little too comfortable." Chandler's eyes widen; he quickly jumps off Joey's lap and says, "All right."
Chandler stands next to Ross. Ross places a hand on Chandler's shoulder as audience laughter is heard.
Ross points at Chandler, gesturing as he speaks. "Okay, look. We have 19 minutes, okay? Chandler? I want you to go and change, okay? And then when you come back, Joey will go change"
Ross points toward Joey and finishes, "and he'll have vacated the chair. Okay?" Chandler nods and replies, "Okay."
Chandler turns away from them and starts walking toward the apartment door.
Chandler pauses, looks back, and says, "All right, fine."
Chandler gestures with his hands and says, "I'm going. When I get back. It's Chair City. And I'm the guy who's sitting in a chair." He then exits through the door.
Phoebe walks out of the hallway wearing a yellow dress. Rachel follows behind her in a robe, holding a sequined garment on a hanger. Ross turns toward them.

Rachel holds up the garment to Ross and asks, "Is this a little too thieves?" Ross looks past her at Phoebe and asks, "What happened?" Phoebe points to a large brown stain on her chest and says, "Hummus. I got the hummus,"
Ross gestures toward Phoebe with his palms up as she touches the stain.

Rachel tells Phoebe, "honey. Well, we'll find you something. Do you want to wear my black jacket?" Ross gestures to Phoebe's dress and says, "That won't go with this dress, though." Rachel replies, "No, you're right." Ross starts, "Well,"

Ross continues, "we'll find you something. Let's just get you out of" Rachel steps forward and reaches for the straps of Phoebe's dress. Ross quickly waves his hands to stop her, saying, "no, no, not out of that. Not out of clothes."
Ross calls out toward the kitchen, "Monica, can Phoebe borrow your green dress?" Monica walks into the living room wearing a slip and says, "I called him."
Ross, Rachel, and Phoebe turn their attention to Monica. Audience laughter is audible.
Monica gestures with her hands as she speaks, "I got his machine, and I left a message. But it's okay. It's okay. It's okay, because you know, it was like a casual, breezy"
"message. It was breezy," Monica says, raising her thumbs.
Monica suddenly grips her hair with both hands.
Monica lowers her hands slightly and says, "Oh, God. What if it wasn't breezy?"
Phoebe gestures with one hand and asks, "Well, how could it not be breezy?" then adds, "No, because you're in such a breezy place."
Monica holds up both index fingers.
"I got it," Monica says, stepping forward. "I will play my message for you guys and you can tell me if it's breezy enough." She walks toward the coffee table.
Monica picks up a cordless phone from the coffee table.
Joey leans forward in his chair and asks, "Monica, how are you going to do that?" Monica holds up the phone and replies, "I know the code to his answering machine."

Ross steps toward Monica, gesturing, and says, "Okay, Mon, I really don't think this is the" Monica sits on the arm of the sofa and begins dialing. Ross points at her and says, "okay, you're dialing. You are dialing."
The apartment door opens, and Chandler enters wearing a tuxedo identical to Ross's. Joey, Ross, and Rachel turn to look at him.
Joey jumps up from his chair, points at Chandler, and charges at him. Chandler turns and runs out the door, with Joey chasing him into the hallway.
Chandler immediately runs back inside the apartment, followed closely by Joey. Joey stops near the door as Chandler moves further in.
Chandler walks over to Rachel and asks, "What's going on?" Rachel replies, "Monica's beeping into Richard's machine." Chandler asks, "Is she crazy?" Phoebe interjects, "Like a straw."
Chandler looks at Phoebe, his brow furrowed.
Phoebe makes a twisting motion with her hands near her head and says, "Remember? Crazy straws."
Chandler stares back at Phoebe without changing his expression.
A man's voice plays from the phone speaker, "Hi. This is Richard."
The recording continues over the phone, "Please leave a message at the tone." Monica holds the phone near her face.
Monica presses a sequence of buttons on the keypad, producing audible dialing beeps.
An automated robotic voice from the phone announces, "You have two new messages."
Joey lowers himself back into the armchair.
Joey nods slowly and says, "What? Cool job."
Joey mimics the automated voice, moving his head stiffly as he repeats, "You have two new messages."
Rachel turns to look at Joey, while Ross stands silently in the background.
Still moving his head stiffly, Joey adds in the same robotic tone, "Please pass the pie."
Joey smiles. A beep sounds from the phone, and Monica presses another button.
Monica's recorded voice plays from the phone speaker, "Hi. It's Monica."

The recording continues, "I'm just checking in because I got this message from you and I didn't know if it was old or new or what. So I'm just checking. So" Present-day Monica stands up and paces toward the kitchen area, while the others watch her.
The recording continues over the speaker, "let me know. Or don't. Whatever." Monica stops pacing.
Monica looks toward the group as the recording pauses.
The recording finishes with, "I'm breezy." Present-day Monica winces, closing her eyes tightly.
Joey raises an arm and points a finger toward Monica from his chair.
Joey lowers his hand and says, "Hey, you can't say you're breezy. That totally negates the breezy."
Monica looks down at the phone. A beep sounds from the device.
A different woman's voice plays from the phone speaker, "Hola, it's me." Monica sets the phone down on the coffee table.
The woman's voice continues, "Yesterday was really fun. Call me about this weekend, okay?" Monica stands completely still, her eyes wide.
Ross's eyes widen. Chandler stands motionless next to Rachel.
Joey gestures with an open hand toward Monica and says, "Now she sounded breezy."
Monica turns her head slowly to stare at Joey without speaking.
The setting changes to an exterior nighttime view of illuminated city skyscrapers.

In an apartment living room, Monica stands near the kitchen area wearing a slip. Ross, Chandler in a tuxedo, Phoebe in a dress, and Rachel in a bathrobe stand around the living area. Monica turns to them with her hands resting on her hips.
Monica gestures with her hands as she says, "He's seeing someone. I can't believe he's seeing someone," followed by audience laughter. Chandler, Phoebe, and Rachel watch her.

Chandler, leaning against the couch, replies, "Monica, you don't know that." Monica asks, "Well, whose voice was that?" Chandler gestures with one hand and says, "Maybe it was his, you know?" as audience laughter sounds.
Ross turns his head slightly toward the group.
Monica looks around and asks, "Michelle?"
Monica steps closer to Rachel and Phoebe.
Monica spreads her arms, saying, "Of course, it was Michelle." She looks at Rachel and asks, "Did it sound like Michelle?"
Rachel raises her fists near her head, her eyes squeezing shut and her face contorting.
Rachel says, "I cannot remember," dropping her hands to her sides as audience laughter is heard.
Monica stares at Rachel. Ross turns toward the window and throws his hands up.
Ross says, "Great. Great. It's starting to rain." He claps his hands together once, then adds, "That'll make it easier to get a cab," his pitch rising slightly, followed by audience laughter.
Chandler shifts his posture as Monica steps closer to him, Phoebe, and Rachel.
Chandler nods and says, "It was Michelle." Monica places a hand on his arm. Rachel nods rapidly and says, "Yeah, it was definitely Michelle."
Rachel points toward the hallway and says, "Okay, Phoebe, you go with Monica."
Phoebe walks past Monica toward the hallway. Rachel gestures with her hands, continuing, "Try on a green dress. If that doesn't work, you wear my gray silk one. Oh, God."
Rachel touches her chest and adds, "Unless I wear it." Ross steps forward, points at her, and says, "Well, you don't know what you're wearing," prompting audience laughter.
Rachel spreads her arms wide.
Rachel says, "Honey, I'm just trying to look nice for your big night," keeping her arms open.
Ross looks down at his wristwatch.

Ross says, "Which we have to leave for in exactly twelve minutes." He gestures toward the hallway, saying, "All right, come on, I'll just pick something out for you." They walk toward a partially open door, and Ross points inside, adding, "Look there. There. That"
Ross finishes, "looks good." Rachel looks into the room and replies, "Ross, that's a towel," followed by audience laughter.
Chandler points a finger to his head. He turns toward Joey, who sits in an armchair wearing a red short-sleeve shirt and jeans.
Chandler says, "Okay."
Chandler gestures to himself and says, "You will notice that I am fully dressed." He gestures toward Joey and adds, "I, in turn, have noticed that you are not."
Chandler points slightly upward, then gestures toward Joey, saying, "So in the words of A.A. Milne, get out of my chair, dill hole," as audience laughter sounds.
Joey stares at Chandler, spreading his arms wide. He then places his hands on the armrests and pushes himself up from the chair.
Joey says, "Okay," as he stands up.
Joey stands, turning his back to Chandler. He grabs the large seat cushion of the armchair and pulls it free.
Joey hoists the seat and back cushions into his arms. He turns and walks toward the kitchen area, carrying them, while Chandler watches with his hands on his hips. Audience laughter sounds.
Chandler follows Joey and asks, "What are you doing?" Joey, holding the cushions, replies, "Well, you said I had to give you the chair. You didn't see anything about the cushions?"
Chandler points a finger at Joey.
Chandler gestures emphatically, saying, "The cushions are the essence of the chair." Joey nods and says, "That's right. I'm taking the essence." over audience laughter.
Joey turns and walks toward his bedroom door.

Joey exits the room. Chandler steps toward the hallway and says, "oh, he'll be back." He glances around the empty living room and adds, "Oh, there's nobody in the room," followed by audience laughter.
Ross walks back into the living area from the hallway.
Rachel follows Ross into the room and says, "I'm sorry. I thought it looked pretty."
Ross sits down on the edge of the coffee table.

Rachel stands with her hands on her hips and says, "Ross, that was a Halloween costume. Unless you would like me to go to the thing as Little Bo Peep," prompting audience laughter. Ross replies, "Look, I didn't recognize it without that"

Ross gestures with his hands, finishing, "inflatable sheep." Rachel turns toward Chandler and says, "Yeah, which, by the way, Chandler, I would like back one of these days," as audience laughter sounds.
Chandler places his hands in his pockets and glances down. Phoebe enters from the hallway, now wearing a yellow sleeveless dress over a lighter slip, adorned with a large flower necklace.
Phoebe approaches Rachel, touching her own chest, and says, "Oh, Rach. Good."

Phoebe gestures to her dress and says, "Listen, isn't this perfect for me?" Rachel replies, "Oh, it's perfect. But not for tonight." Phoebe nods, adding, "Well, no, of course not for tonight." Rachel agrees, "Yeah, hi."
Ross stands up quickly from the coffee table.

Ross steps toward Phoebe and Rachel, raising his hands, and says, "Tonight not for tonight. What are you doing?" Rachel waves her hands back and forth, "No, I'm sorry." Ross lowers his hands repeatedly, "Wait a minute. I love you. I love you. Breathe."
Ross turns and walks away from them. Chandler steps forward, looking at Rachel.
Chandler says, "We used them as pillows when we went camping," followed by audience laughter.
Ross turns back toward Chandler.
Ross asks, "What?"
Chandler looks back at Ross.
Chandler replies, "The sheep," prompting audience laughter.
Ross stares at Chandler, blinking.
Ross raises his hands with palms facing forward and says, "Hey, what you do on your own time?"
Chandler stands silently. Joey opens his bedroom door and steps out, pointing a finger forward. The screen cuts to black.
The screen remains black.
